Hi Chris,

I wanted to flag some issues I noticed on two of our pages that may be affecting user experience. On both pages, the footer content does not align with the main topic of the page:

  • Add cities/regions naturally - especially the 5 GBP's we have.

Thanks much!

title: Christian: Hatch Integration via Harness
description:
Hi Keith,

Seeking help if we can add this to the Christian site. I have added the client email info below:

Here’s a simplified guide for enabling the Gravity Forms API and generating the necessary API keys:

Step 1: Log in to WordPress Admin
Go to https://fhfurr.com/wp-admin. Log in with your WordPress credentials.


Step 2: Enable the Gravity Forms API
In the WordPress admin dashboard, navigate to Forms > Settings. Click the REST API tab at the top. Check the box to Enable access to the API. Click Save Settings.


Step 3: Generate API Keys
On the REST API settings page, click Add Key. Fill out the following:
• Description: Enter a name (e.g., “Hatch Integration”).
• User: Select the WordPress user to associate with the API key.
• Permissions: Choose Read/Write.
Click Add Key.


Step 4: Copy the API Credentials
After creating the key, you’ll see:
• Consumer Key
• Consumer Secret
Copy these credentials immediately and store them securely. The Consumer Secret cannot be viewed again after you leave this page.


Step 5: Note the API Endpoint
The API endpoint URL is typically: https://fhfurr.com/wp-json/gf/v2/

Thanks much.

1. Make copies of...

Client Workbook
Client Report - Not currently used
GeoGrid Datasheets
GeoGrid Charts
Project Roadmap
New Client Onboarding Form - Created automatically

2. Rename them by replacing "Project Number" with actual project number from the Project Register (column D)

3. Place the Project Roadmap & New Client Onboarding Form PLUS auto-generated form folder in the Onboarding folder

title: Copy empty project folders to Client Folder
Create Client Folder in CLIXSY Projects Google Drive
Check all required fields in Enquiry form are complete
Create Basecamp project based on latest appropriate template
Raise Project number in Project Register
description:
1. Change column A from Inactive to Active

  2. Change column E from Active to Inactive

  3. Add entry to column B:
    3.1 Copy Enquiry Number from column E and 'paste special'>'values only' into column B
    3.2 Remove "E" prefix and replace with:
         J, if operation (has no agreed end date, SEO, LSA etc.)
         P, if project (has agreed end date, website development etc.)
         3.21 Change box background color:
                      Green: SEO
                      Yellow: LSA
                      Blue: WebDev

  4. Add project start date in column C - Should usually be today's date
         An easy way to do this would be to copy the date from the cell above, double click and choose today's date from the calendar date picker

  5. Select the cell above in column D and pull the handle down.
         This will automatically add the correct project title column D. This is what you use for the project number in the project doc titles
